name: Morón de la Frontera from Utrera – circular via El Coronil
name_original: Morón de la Frontera desde Utrera – circular por El Coronil
region_intro: Road cycling around Utrera offers a varied landscape characterized by its Mediterranean environment, featuring both flat sections and challenging climbs. The region southeast of Seville provides an extensive network of routes that traverse agricultural fields, natural reserves, and areas around reservoirs. This terrain allows for diverse road cycling experiences, from routes suitable for speed to paths with significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Utrera The most popular road cycling route is Morón de la Frontera from Utrera – circular via El Coronil, a 53.0 miles (85.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route features over 1,800 feet (566 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Utrera to Villamartín – circular via El Coronil and Puerto Serrano, a difficult 73.2 miles (117.9 km) path. This route includes over 3,400 feet (1,041 meters) of climbing, traversing varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Church of Santiago, Utrera – The Thinker loop from Utrera, a 34.7 miles (55.8 km) trail leading through the Utrera area, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Utrera is defined by its Mediterranean landscape, agricultural fields, and the presence of natural reserves and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Utrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Utrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Utrera?
A: Utrera offers a diverse selection of nearly 30 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable path for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Utrera?
A: The road cycling routes around Utrera feature a varied Mediterranean landscape. You'll encounter both relatively flat sections, ideal for speed, and challenging climbs that will test your endurance. The terrain often traverses agricultural fields, natural reserves, and areas around reservoirs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Utrera?
A: The best time for road cycling in Utrera is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. While winter offers pleasant conditions, summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable during those months.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Utrera?
A: Yes, Utrera offers several routes suitable for beginners or families. There are 3 easy routes and 18 moderate routes available, providing options that don't require extensive experience or high fitness levels. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40340658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago, Utrera – The Thinker loop from Utrera&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 55.8 km long.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling experience in Utrera?
A: The road cycling experience in Utrera is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic Mediterranean landscape, and the extensive network of routes that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles I can visit along the routes?
A: Yes, Utrera's routes can lead you past several historical landmarks. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Utrera&lt;/a&gt;, or other notable sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2778669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hacienda de Gandul&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself also boasts elegant ancestral homes, Gothic churches, and noble palaces.

Q: Can I explore natural reserves or scenic viewpoints while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region features significant natural areas like the Utrera Natural Reserve, known for its saltwater lakes and diverse bird species. You can also cycle towards the scenic Embalse de Torre del Águila (Torre del Águila Reservoir), which offers beautiful views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994324788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morón de la Frontera from Utrera – circular via El Coronil&lt;/a&gt; traverse varied landscapes that include natural beauty.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Utrera?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Utrera are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994324788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morón de la Frontera from Utrera – circular via El Coronil&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995751329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Utrera to Villamartín – circular via El Coronil and Puerto Serrano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is it possible to reach the cycling routes using public transport with my bike?
A: Utrera is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train from Seville. While specific rules for bikes on public transport can vary, trains in Andalusia often accommodate bicycles, especially outside of peak hours. It's advisable to check with the local transport provider for the most current regulations regarding bikes.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Utrera?
A: Utrera offers various parking options within the town, including street parking and public car parks. Many cyclists choose to park near the town center or at the start points of popular routes, which are often accessible from main roads. Consolation Park, a lovely green space, could also be a convenient starting point with potential parking nearby.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Utrera provides several challenging routes for experienced road cyclists. There are 6 difficult routes, featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995751329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Utrera to Villamartín – circular via El Coronil and Puerto Serrano&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 118 km with over 1,000 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I find bike-friendly cafes or places to rest along the routes?
A: Utrera and the surrounding towns along the routes offer various cafes and establishments where cyclists can take a break, refuel, and enjoy local refreshments. Many villages you pass through will have small bars or cafes, especially in their main squares, perfect for a mid-ride stop.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the road cycling trails?
A: Given the region's natural reserves and Mediterranean landscape, you might encounter various bird species, especially around the Utrera Natural Reserve's saltwater lakes. These include coots, mallards, flamingos, and purple herons, particularly during winter and spring. The agricultural fields and natural areas also host other local fauna.
tips: It is a 14th century fortress of Muslim origin, modified in the 15th and 16th centuries. Its mission was to defend the spring of water from the source of the Aguzaderas or the Abuzadera. It is the only castle located in a hollow surrounded by hills. It is part of the Route of the Castles Witnesses of the Reconquest.

The castle of Morón de la Frontera is a defensive complex from the 11th or 12th century, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest on the Historical Heritage of Spain. The keep, called Torre Gorda, is one of the most important elements of the building. In 1810 the castle served Napoleon's army as a fortress in the battle against the troops of Ronda.

Alcazaba de Utrera is a 13th century fortification, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1985. On the highest terrace, next to the gate, is the Torre del Homenaje, which was set up as a museum space around the history of the castle. The hours are from 10:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. and from 4:30 p.m. to 8:30 p.m.

waypoints.name: Castle of Morón de la Frontera
